original_hypothesis.title: PrizePool closeDraw truncates contributions to uint96, potentially losing prize liquidity distribution for large pools
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: PrizePool.closeDraw casts `_contributionsForDraw(...)` (uint256) to uint96 before passing into _nextDraw. If contributions exceed type(uint96).max, the value truncates and excess contributed prize tokens are not distributed into tiers/reserve as intended, leaving tokens stranded as unaccounted balance and breaking economic expectations.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Although large contributions are required, this is a concrete code defect that can produce accounting divergence and operational disputes once TVL/volume is high (or if prizeToken has low decimals causing large raw amounts).
deployment_assumptions: Per-draw prize token contributions can exceed uint96 max in raw units (depends on token decimals and scale)
Protocol does not cap contributions before they reach PrizePool
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No deployment configuration provided on expected contribution ceilings; verify prizeToken decimals and expected max per-draw contributions.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Unsafe downcast uint256 -> uint96 on contributions

preconditions: Total contributions for a draw exceed type(uint96).max
Users attempt to claim prizes expecting full distribution

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Accumulator tracks uint256 but distribution path uses uint96

operational_questions: What is the realistic upper bound of per-draw prizeToken contributions for target deployments?
Does the prize token have 6 decimals (raising truncation likelihood in raw units)?
Would stranded tokens be reclaimable or permanently stuck as unallocated balance?
validation_requirements: Property test: force contributions > uint96 max, closeDraw, and verify mismatch between PrizePool.balance and computed distributions
Check whether accountedBalance/solvency invariant fails or silently drifts

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Accumulate or flash-source extremely large prizeToken amounts and contribute to PrizePool for a draw
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Wait for closeDraw; confirm truncation and misdistribution
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Large capital; not typical attacker path

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Distribution errors, accounting disputes, potential insolvency perception
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium (may require manual recovery process if any exists)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Draw funding and prize distribution correctness

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Very large ContributePrizeTokens events in a single draw
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: PrizePool prizeToken balance grows but reserve/tier allocations do not

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
